title_en: Pushing the Boundaries of Indie Comics: Matt Kindt’s Acclaimed Flux House Imprint Announces a New Expansion Packed With Mind-Bending Stories
summary_en: Matt Kindt's Flux House imprint is one of the most exciting things happening in comics right now, and it's getting even bigger later in 2026.
body_en:
Creator Matt Kindt's Flux House imprint has quickly developed into one of the most exciting new publishing initiatives in the comic book world, and it's only getting bigger. IGN can exclusively reveal the first details about Flux House Presents, a new anthology series designed to coexist with ongoing titles like Mind MGMT: New & Improved and Fort Psycho.
Check out the slideshow gallery below for a closer look at Flux House Presents #1:
Flux House Presents is a quarterly anthology series intended to release in between new arcs of Mind MGMT: New & Improved. The first issue features three new stories from creators like Brian Hurtt, David Lapham, Alison Sampson, and Ella Kindt.
Here's the official summary of Flux House Presents #1:
“I really miss the weird anthology. From the classic DHP to the one-man show of Eightball, Negative Burn and Taboo,” said Kindt in a statement. “I want to channel that weird unexpected fun on every page with pulp and horror and sci-fi. I want to bring back short form comics — where even a 2-page story can change your life. And give creators a chance to play and experiment.”
Flux House Presents #1 will be released on October 7, 2026. You can preorder a copy at your local comic shop .
Fans attending SDCC next week can get a taste of what's to come at the “Inside FLUX HOUSE with Matt Kindt and Brian Hurtt” panel. The panel will be held on Saturday, June 25th at 1 pm PT in Room 5AB.
SDCC 2026 will be held from Wednesday, July 22 through Sunday, July 26. For more on what to expect from the show, check out Funko's exclusive Invincible Pop! figures and Jazware's exclusive collectibles .
Jesse is a mild-mannered staff writer for IGN. Allow him to lend a machete to your intellectual thicket by following @jschedeen on BlueSky .
